content.problemStatement:
Chef has planned that he will drink exactly XXX liters of tea daily. He has an **empty** jar having a capacity of YYY liters.

Chef can visit the tea shop to refill the jar. In each refill, the jar is completely filled to the brim and Chef is charged ZZZ rupees.

Chef wonders what is the **minimum** amount of money he has to pay for drinking exactly XXX liters of tea daily.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, three space-separated integers XXX, YYY, and ZZZ denoting the amount of tea Chef has to drink daily, the capacity of his jar and the cost of refilling his jar.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the minimum amount Chef has to pay.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can get his jar refilled once and drink 333 liters of tea, which will cost him 333 rupees in total.
Test case 222: Chef needs to refill his jar twice to get 3⋅2=63\cdot 2 = 63⋅2=6 liters of tea, which will cost him 3⋅2=63\cdot 2 = 63⋅2=6 rupees in total.
Test case 333: Chef can refill his jar once and drink exactly 555 liters out of the 777 liters. This will cost him 222 rupees.
Test case 444: Chef can refill his jar twice and drink 4⋅2=84\cdot 2 = 84⋅2=8 liters of tea. He needs to have one more refill, but this time he would drink only 111 liter tea from the jar. Thus the total tea he drinks is 4+4+1=94+4+1 = 94+4+1=9 liters. He made 333 refills in total costing him 5⋅3=155\cdot 3 = 155⋅3=15 rupees.
